Operation 'deleteSubscription' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Current description is too minimal. Added clarity about the side effects (stopping notifications, permanent removal), the irreversibility of the action, and what happens to the subscription's configuration. This helps AI agents understand the consequences of using this operation and when it's appropriate to call it.

data.description_suggestion: Delete an existing subscription and stop receiving event notifications. This operation permanently removes the subscription and all associated webhook configurations. After deletion, no further event notifications will be sent to the subscription's notification URL. This action cannot be undone.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal. Added clarity about the side effects (stopping notifications, permanent removal), the irreversibility of the action, and what happens to the subscription's configuration. This helps AI agents understand the consequences of using this operation and when it's appropriate to call it.

message: Operation 'getEventsForSubscription' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary 'Get list of event notifications' is too generic and doesn't specify the subscription context. Description is verbose but lacks clarity on what the response contains. Improved summary specifies the subscription context. Improved description clarifies the operation's purpose (monitoring/troubleshooting), consolidates pagination details more clearly, and explicitly states what is returned for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
Get a list of event notifications sent to you for a subscription. You can use this endpoint to address any issues with notification retries.

In the request, set `createdTimeFrom` and `createdTimeTo` to get event notifications for the specified time range. If you do not set `createdTimeTo`, the current date and time is used. By default, you get 20 results on one page of results. Set `max` in your request to get up to 100 results on one page.

**Note**: In this list operation, pagination is available only in one direction. The `nextPage` value is available in the response for you to retrieve the next page of results. However, you cannot retrieve the previous page of results.

See [Webhooks](doc:webhooks) in the Guides section for more information, sample requests, and notification payloads for BILL events.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve event notifications for a subscription
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a list of event notifications that were sent to a specific subscription. This endpoint is useful for monitoring notification delivery and troubleshooting failed notifications. Supports filtering by creation time range using `createdTimeFrom` and `createdTimeTo` parameters. Returns paginated results (default 20 per page, max 100). Pagination is forward-only; use the `nextPage` value from the response to retrieve subsequent pages. Returns a list of event notifications with retry information.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Get list of event notifications' is too generic and doesn't specify the subscription context. Description is verbose but lacks clarity on what the response contains. Improved summary specifies the subscription context. Improved description clarifies the operation's purpose (monitoring/troubleshooting), consolidates pagination details more clearly, and explicitly states what is returned for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'resendEvent' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is too vague and doesn't indicate this is a manual retry operation. Description lacks clarity on what happens when the operation executes and what is returned. Improved summary specifies 'manually resend' and 'failed' to clarify the use case. Improved description explains the side effect (notification sent to endpoint), clarifies the return value, and distinguishes manual retry from automatic retry behavior for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
Resend an existing event notification.

**Note**: When an event notification fails, BILL tries to send the notification again with exponential backoff.

See [Webhooks](doc:webhooks) in the Guides section for more information, sample requests, and notification payloads for BILL events.
data.summary_suggestion: Manually resend a failed event notification
data.description_suggestion: Manually resend an event notification for a specific subscription. This operation allows you to retry sending a notification that previously failed or was not delivered. The notification will be sent to the subscription's configured endpoint. Returns the event details and resend status. Note: BILL automatically retries failed notifications with exponential backoff; this endpoint provides manual retry capability.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too vague and doesn't indicate this is a manual retry operation. Description lacks clarity on what happens when the operation executes and what is returned. Improved summary specifies 'manually resend' and 'failed' to clarify the use case. Improved description explains the side effect (notification sent to endpoint), clarifies the return value, and distinguishes manual retry from automatic retry behavior for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'getAllSubscriptions' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary 'Get list of subscriptions' is generic and doesn't specify event subscriptions. Description is verbose with repetitive pagination notes. Improved summary clarifies these are event subscriptions. Improved description consolidates pagination information more concisely and explicitly states what subscription details are returned, improving AI agent understanding of the response content.

data.current_description:
Get a list of all your created subscriptions.

By default, you get 20 results on one page of results. Set `max` in your request to get up to 100 results on one page.

**Note**: In this list operation, pagination is available only in one direction. The `nextPage` value is available in the response for you to retrieve the next page of results. However, you cannot retrieve the previous page of results.

See [Webhooks](doc:webhooks) in the Guides section for more information, sample requests, and notification payloads for BILL events.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a list of all event subscriptions you have created. Returns paginated results with a default of 20 subscriptions per page (maximum 100). Pagination is forward-only; use the `nextPage` value from the response to retrieve subsequent pages. Each subscription includes its configuration, status, and associated events.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Get list of subscriptions' is generic and doesn't specify event subscriptions. Description is verbose with repetitive pagination notes. Improved summary clarifies these are event subscriptions. Improved description consolidates pagination information more concisely and explicitly states what subscription details are returned, improving AI agent understanding of the response content.

message: Operation 'createSubscription' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about what inputs are required (name, URL, event types), what is returned (subscription ID, security key), and side effects (subscription becomes active, notifications begin). Improved description specifies required inputs, clarifies the return value including security key, and explains the side effect of immediate activation for better AI agent understanding of the operation's full lifecycle.

data.current_description:
Create a new event subscription. You can subscribe to multiple events with a single request.

BILL provides a full catalog of all events that you can subscribe to with `GET /v3/events/catalog`.

See [Webhooks](doc:webhooks) in the Guides section for more information, sample requests, and notification payloads for BILL events.
data.description_suggestion: Create a new event subscription to receive webhook notifications for specified events. You can subscribe to multiple events in a single request. Requires a subscription name, target URL endpoint, and list of event types from the event catalog (retrieve available events via GET /v3/events/catalog). Returns the created subscription details including subscription ID, security key for message signing, and configuration. The subscription becomes active immediately and will begin sending notifications to the specified endpoint.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about what inputs are required (name, URL, event types), what is returned (subscription ID, security key), and side effects (subscription becomes active, notifications begin). Improved description specifies required inputs, clarifies the return value including security key, and explains the side effect of immediate activation for better AI agent understanding of the operation's full lifecycle.

message: Operation 'getSubscription' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is too vague and doesn't specify what details are returned. Improved description explicitly lists the types of information returned (configuration, endpoint, events, status, security key, timestamps) to help AI agents understand what data they will receive and when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Get details about an existing subscription.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve detailed information about a specific event subscription by subscription ID. Returns the subscription configuration including name, target endpoint URL, list of subscribed events, current status, security key, and creation/modification timestamps.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is too vague and doesn't specify what details are returned. Improved description explicitly lists the types of information returned (configuration, endpoint, events, status, security key, timestamps) to help AI agents understand what data they will receive and when to use this operation.

Enhanced version clarifies the permanence of deletion, explains the side effect (stops webhook notifications), indicates what is removed (subscription and history), and notes the irreversibility. This helps AI agents understand the consequences and determine if this operation matches their intent.

data.description_suggestion: Permanently delete an existing subscription and stop receiving webhook notifications. This operation removes the subscription and all associated event delivery history. Once deleted, the subscription cannot be recovered. Requires the subscription ID and appropriate authentication.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal. Enhanced version clarifies the permanence of deletion, explains the side effect (stops webhook notifications), indicates what is removed (subscription and history), and notes the irreversibility. message: Operation 'sendTestEvent' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Current description lacks clarity about what happens as a result of the operation (where the notification is sent, when it's sent). Enhanced version explicitly states the notification is sent to the subscription's URL, clarifies the optional nature of fields with examples, emphasizes the test-only nature of data, and explains the practical use case. This helps AI agents understand the operation's effects and when it's appropriate to use.

data.current_description:
Generate a new test event notification. After you create a subscription, use this endpoint for BILL to send a test event notification to your specified notification URL.

In your request, all the fields are optional. To see specific information in the test event notification, you can set `eventType`, `version`, and any details. You can get the BILL events catalog with `GET /v3/events/catalog`.

**Note**: All the fields in the test event notification are test values. You cannot use any of the test values for any other operation.

See [Webhooks](doc:webhooks) in the Guides section for more information, sample requests, and notification payloads for BILL events.
data.description_suggestion: Generate and send a test event notification to the subscription's notification URL to validate webhook integration. All request fields are optional; specify `eventType`, `version`, and other details to customize the test payload. The test notification is sent immediately to the configured `notificationUrl`. Important: All values in test notifications are synthetic test data and cannot be used in other operations. Use `GET /v3/events/catalog` to discover available event types. This operation is useful for testing webhook delivery and payload handling before production use.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks clarity about what happens as a result of the operation (where the notification is sent, when it's sent). Enhanced version explicitly states the notification is sent to the subscription's URL, clarifies the optional nature of fields with examples, emphasizes the test-only nature of data, and explains the practical use case. This helps AI agents understand the operation's effects and when it's appropriate to use.

data.current_description:
Get a list of all your created subscriptions.

By default, you get 20 results on one page of results. Set `max` in your request to get up to 100 results on one page.

**Note**: In this list operation, pagination is available only in one direction. The `nextPage` value is available in the response for you to retrieve the next page of results. However, you cannot retrieve the previous page of results.

See [Webhooks](doc:webhooks) in the Guides section for more information, sample requests, and notification payloads for BILL events.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a list of all event subscriptions you have created. Returns paginated results with a default of 20 subscriptions per page (maximum 100). Pagination is forward-only; use the `nextPage` value from the response to retrieve subsequent pages. Each subscription includes its configuration, status, and associated events.

Improved summary clarifies these are event subscriptions. Improved description consolidates pagination information more concisely and explicitly states what subscription details are returned, improving AI agent understanding of the response content.

message: Operation 'createSubscription' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about what inputs are required (name, URL, event types), what is returned (subscription ID, security key), and side effects (subscription becomes active, notifications begin). data.current_description:
Create a new event subscription. You can subscribe to multiple events with a single request.

BILL provides a full catalog of all events that you can subscribe to with `GET /v3/events/catalog`.

See [Webhooks](doc:webhooks) in the Guides section for more information, sample requests, and notification payloads for BILL events.
data.description_suggestion: Create a new event subscription to receive webhook notifications for specified events. You can subscribe to multiple events in a single request. Requires a subscription name, target URL endpoint, and list of event types from the event catalog (retrieve available events via GET /v3/events/catalog). Returns the created subscription details including subscription ID, security key for message signing, and configuration. The subscription becomes active immediately and will begin sending notifications to the specified endpoint.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about what inputs are required (name, URL, event types), what is returned (subscription ID, security key), and side effects (subscription becomes active, notifications begin). Improved description specifies required inputs, clarifies the return value including security key, and explains the side effect of immediate activation for better AI agent understanding of the operation's full lifecycle.
